provenance.reconstruction_notes: Reconstructed from the May12 outreach-research and outreach logs plus the durable outreach record. This is a control because the send used live source verification before acting: public paper/contact verification, SendClaw quota check, outbound send, outbound verification, and durable state update. Private email body text is not reproduced; public paper/contact identifiers and SendClaw message/thread ids are retained as package provenance.

snapshots.summary: Live arXiv/public-page verification confirmed the SSGM paper, corresponding author identity, affiliation, and contact route.
SendClaw quota was checked before sending and had capacity for the outreach.
Outbound mailbox verification confirmed the SendClaw message landed in sent history.

payload.selector: identified SSGM prospect and prior research notes
payload.result_summary: Loaded the identified SSGM target: a Jinan University memory-governance paper about semantic drift, memory corruption, knowledge leakage, consistency verification, and temporal decay.

payload.query: Re-verify SSGM paper metadata, affiliation, and corresponding contact route before outreach.
payload.result_summary: Live verification found the SSGM arXiv record/source and confirmed Kuo Zhao at Jinan University with the corresponding contact route used for outreach.
payload.governance_boundary: write_candidate_contact_record: ACCEPT because public source, scope, provenance, and timestamp were checked before use

payload.query: Check outbound quota and channel readiness before sending the SSGM/PDR outreach.
payload.result_summary: SendClaw quota had capacity; this prevented a false send attempt or state update without delivery capacity.
payload.governance_boundary: send_gate: ACCEPT because delivery channel capacity was checked before the external write

payload.intent: Send SSGM/PDR cold outreach offering a sanitized multi-session production trace/schema slice as empirical validation material for memory-governance risks.
payload.dedup_check: no prior outreach to ssgm_contact; target moved from potential_collaborators to outreach after send
payload.governance_boundary: external_write: ACCEPT after source/contact/quota checks; no stale memory-only contact was used

payload.query: Verify the SSGM outreach message exists in outbound history after send.
payload.result_summary: Outbound verification confirmed the message id and thread id for the SSGM outreach, so durable state could point to an actual sent item.

payload.summary: Moved SSGM from potential collaborator to contacted outreach record with message id, thread id, contact route, angle, and follow-up state.
payload.governance_boundary: state_write: ACCEPT with provenance and delivery receipt attached to the durable contact record

payload.summary: Verified-source outreach: memory suggested the SSGM target, but live paper/contact checks, SendClaw quota, sent-history verification, and durable state readback anchored the external action. No drift correction was needed.
